description: Sema Mapping Spine, Slice 0 — take ONCOTREE_CODE -> condition_concept_id from a hardened source semantic graph through a resolved value-mapping store to a compiled, QA'd staging artifact in Databricks, measured against a hand-labelled gold set. OMOP/cBioPortal is the showcase; every story keeps OMOP specifics behind the R29 policy/manifest boundary. See tasks/prd-sema-mapping-slice0.md for the full contract (§1.5) and rationale.

Depends on US-000 (guard must be green on the current tree the first time it runs)

Missing core paths (resolve/engine.py, compile/, resolve/assembler.py) are skipped so the guard tightens automatically as later stories create them. mypy clean, coverage 93%.

userStories.title: Slice-0 OncoTree->SNOMED gold set + mapping eval harness
userStories.description: As the team, I want every distinct ONCOTREE_CODE hand-labelled to its correct standard SNOMED concept_id (or NO_MAP) with an eval harness scoring the §1.5 confusion matrix, so 'done' is defined before the resolver exists.
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test first: synthetic gold + decision set with known Zones -> mapped_precision / mapped_recall / auto_resolution_rate / no_map_accuracy compute to the §1.5(f) values; a predicted NO_MAP is TN when gold=NO_MAP and FN (recall miss) when gold is a real concept; no_map_accuracy reported SEPARATELY

Gold set artifact tests/data/gold/oncotree_condition_slice0.jsonl: one row per distinct ONCOTREE_CODE, columns oncotree_code, gold_concept_id, gold_label (RESOLVED|NO_MAP), row_count, notes; distinct codes enumerated from ~/.sema/poc.duckdb cbioportal_* (document the query)

HUMAN-LABEL CHECKPOINT (NOT Ralph-automatable): gold_concept_id is an external oracle — hand-labelled by a human or trusted crosswalk, NEVER generated by Sema's own resolver (else US-012 is circular). Ralph MAY scaffold the file, enumerate distinct codes, fill row_count/notes, and mark passes:true on harness + scaffold + documented subset; completing 100% distinct-code coverage is a HUMAN gate US-012 acceptance depends on. Surface the unlabelled remainder; never invent labels

Eval harness src/sema/eval/mapping_goldset.py (+ *_utils.py) implements the §1.5(f) confusion matrix exactly; unit of evaluation = distinct source code; reports distinct-code, row-weighted, and per-frequency-bucket numbers
integration live test (skip-guarded on ~/.sema/poc.duckdb): assert the gold set covers every observed ONCOTREE_CODE (or records it as a known-unlabelled gap)
This story ships NO resolver — only the gold set + harness
Conforms to PRD §1.5 Contract Spec ((f) confusion matrix); do NOT restate columns — the US-005 drift test enforces the frozen schema

userStories.notes: Harness: src/sema/eval/mapping_goldset_utils.py (frozen §1.5(f) math: derive_zone/classify_cell/ConfusionMatrix incl mapped_precision/recall/auto_resolution_rate/no_map_accuracy-separate) + mapping_goldset.py (load_gold_set, GoldSet labelled/unlabelled accounting, score() at distinct-code/row-weighted/per-bucket). Artifact tests/data/gold/oncotree_condition_slice0.jsonl scaffolds all 64 distinct ONCOTREE_CODE (gbm_tcga_pan_can_atlas_2018 + msk_chord_2024 sample tables; documented enumeration query) with row_count filled. HUMAN GATE: 0/64 hand-labelled (gold_label=UNLABELLED) — Ralph did NOT invent labels (would make US-012 circular); remainder surfaced via GoldSet.unlabelled_codes(). 29 hermetic unit tests assert exact §1.5 values; skip-guarded integration test confirms 64/64 coverage + row_count match vs live poc.duckdb. No resolver shipped. mypy clean, coverage 93% (98% new modules).

userStories.title: Per-vocabulary resolver policy object (OMOP/OncoTree behind the boundary)
userStories.description: As the engine, I want OMOP specifics captured in a policy object so the resolver stays vocabulary-agnostic and source-vs-target conflation (R9) is structurally impossible. Built before the vocab store so the store consumes policy literals.
acceptanceCriteria: Consume the merged VocabularyBindingDecl (models/target/vocab_binding.py): read domain/require_standard/allow_zero_default from the loaded binding; resolver_policy_ref resolves to this policy. Do NOT duplicate the binding's fields. Cite the file in progress.txt

Failing tests first: a ResolverPolicy instance exposes source_vocabulary (OncoTree), maps_to_relationship ('Maps to'), standard_flag ('S'), target_domain (Condition), allow_zero_default (per-obligation, default off), and a validity predicate; the engine code never names these literals
src/sema/resolve/policy.py holds the dataclass; the OMOP/OncoTree instance lives in allowlisted src/sema/resolve/policies/omop.py (the only place these literals may appear)

R9 distinction: resolver_policy_ref names the SOURCE vocabulary (OncoTree); the contract's singular vocabulary_ref is the TARGET (SNOMED). A test asserts the resolver matches the source code in the SOURCE vocabulary at candidate-generation time
R29 guard (US-001) stays green: no 'Maps to'/standard_concept/concept_id/OMOP table/domain/OncoTree literal in src/sema/engine/ or any generic spine module

userStories.notes: ResolverPolicy (src/sema/resolve/policy.py) is vocabulary-agnostic: SOURCE-side literals (source_vocabulary/maps_to_relationship/standard_flag) are data; target obligation (target_domain/require_standard/allow_zero_default) is read via @property from the bound VocabularyBindingDecl (models/target/vocab_binding.py) — never duplicated. Validity predicate accepts()=valid AND (standard if required) AND in-target-domain; candidate_lookup() matches the SOURCE vocab (R9). OMOP/OncoTree instance + 'Maps to'/'S' literals confined to allowlisted resolve/policies/omop.py; resolve/policies/__init__.py resolve_policy(binding) dispatches on resolver_policy_ref (UnknownResolverPolicyError otherwise). 13 hermetic unit tests (TDD). R29 guard clean, mypy clean, coverage 93% repo / 100% new modules.

src/sema/resolve/vocab_store.py: a VocabStore reading concept, concept_relationship, concept_synonym (+ domain); backend selected by config (DuckDB ~/.sema/poc.duckdb or Databricks workspace.vocabulary_omop)
SQL built as a SQLGlot AST rendered per dialect (sqlglot>=25.0.0, pyproject.toml:22); no hand-concatenated SQL strings
No OMOP literals beyond table/column names passed in as config; relationship name ('Maps to') and standard flag come from the US-004 policy

Generic VocabStoreSchema (role-based field names) + ConceptRow (id/name/domain/vocabulary/standard/code/invalid_reason); OMOP column literals confined to policies/omop.py:OMOP_VOCAB_SCHEMA (R29-allowlisted). _fetch handles DuckDB (result.fetchall) and DBAPI cursors (conn.fetchall) so the same class serves Databricks in US-013. 16 hermetic unit tests (fake conn records SQL+params) TDD-first. Live integration (skip-guarded poc.duckdb): LUAD->777926 domain Condition; maps_to standard SNOMED Condition 45768916; latency ~0.5s (<30s bound) on 54.9M concept_relationship. userStories.title: Resolved value-mapping store (DuckDB table, §1.5 frozen columns)
userStories.description: As the compiler, I want a concrete executable store of source_code -> concept_id decisions with the §1.5(a) frozen columns and location-independent grain, so there is somewhere to read resolved decisions from.
acceptanceCriteria: Failing tests first: write N distinct-code decisions, read back; grain enforced as one row per §1.5(a) grain key (a second write for the same key upserts, never duplicates); NO_MAP rows persist with concept_id=NULL and a no_map_reason; status round-trips
src/sema/resolve/value_mapping_store.py: DuckDB-backed table with the EXACT §1.5(a) column list (location-independent — no source_schema/source_table)
SCHEMA DRIFT TEST (freezes the contract): an integration test asserts the live table's column set equals the §1.5(a) frozen list exactly — adding/removing/renaming a column fails
Canonical home is DuckDB; no Databricks seed sync (the compiler inlines decisions, US-010); document this in the module docstring
No SSSOM dependency (sssom-py)
integration live test: round-trip a handful of real resolved codes against a temp DuckDB file; assert the schema matches the frozen list
Conforms to PRD §1.5 Contract Spec ((a) value-mapping store); do NOT restate columns — the US-005 drift test enforces the frozen schema
R29 guard (US-001) stays green: no 'Maps to'/standard_concept/concept_id/OMOP table/domain/OncoTree literal in src/sema/engine/ or any generic spine module

userStories.notes: ValueMappingStore (src/sema/resolve/value_mapping_store.py) is a DuckDB-backed §1.5(a) decision cache; value_mapping_store_utils.py owns the FROZEN_COLUMNS (15) + GRAIN_KEY (5) constants, the ValueMapping frozen dataclass (concept_id NULL iff NO_MAP; no_map_reason non-null iff NO_MAP; confidence in [0,1]), and the SQL builders (create/upsert/select). Table PRIMARY KEY = the §1.5(a) grain (source_vocabulary, normalized_source_value, target_property_ref, resolver_policy_ref, vocab_release); upsert uses DuckDB ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE so a re-write of the same grain updates, never duplicates; vocab_release is part of the grain (different release => distinct row); run_id is provenance-only. Location-independent (NO source_schema/source_table). ResolutionStatus enum defined locally in resolve (mirrors eval's; identical values, no eval edit). DuckDB is the canonical home, no Databricks seed sync (compiler inlines, US-010) — documented in the module docstring. No SSSOM dep. 20 hermetic unit tests (temp DuckDB) + integration drift/round-trip test (tests/integration/test_value_mapping_store_schema.py, temp DuckDB so always runnable under -m integration). mypy clean (186 files); R29 guard green; coverage 93% repo / 94% store / 98% utils. US-006 will be the sole writer.

userStories.title: Vocabulary resolver (the §4 algorithm)
userStories.description: As Sema, I want the deterministic resolver that turns a distinct source code into a standard target concept_id (or NO_MAP), so VOCAB_LOOKUP values become real decisions.
acceptanceCriteria: Failing tests first (mocked VocabStore + policy), one per stage: (1a) exact code match in the SOURCE vocabulary; (2) 'Maps to' standardization to standard_concept='S', keeping only invalid_reason IS NULL and validity-window concepts; (3) domain gate rejects any candidate whose domain_id != target_domain; emit MappingAssertion(pattern=VOCAB_LOOKUP, status=auto_accepted) on one survivor; first-class NO_MAP (resolution_status=NO_MAP, Zone-3) when zero standard candidates survive

src/sema/resolve/ modules each <=400 lines: candidates.py, standardize.py, domain_gate.py, engine.py (orchestration -> emits assertions); disambiguate.py present but SINGLE-MODEL only (no council). The hot path (ONCOTREE_CODE) is pure SQL — invokes no LLM; the LLM fires only on a genuine >1-survivor tie (-> Zone-2)
Code-bearing short-circuit: for code-bearing inputs the engine takes the SQL path and never touches embeddings/vector recall

US-006 is the SOLE writer of value-mapping-store rows (US-005): one row per §1.5(a) grain key incl NO_MAP, carrying status + resolution_status. US-009/US-010/US-011/US-012 READ the store, never write it

integration live test (~/.sema/poc.duckdb): resolve a real sample of OncoTree codes; LUAD and other gold-labelled codes resolve to their gold concept_id; >=1 known dead-end code yields NO_MAP; measure and RECORD OncoTree->SNOMED path coverage (reported here, threshold asserted in US-012)
R29 guard (US-001) stays green: no 'Maps to'/standard_concept/concept_id/OMOP table/domain/OncoTree literal in src/sema/engine/ or any generic spine module
Strict TDD: failing test(s) written first, failing for the right reason

Append a Learnings entry to progress.txt; update .wolf/ (memory.md, anatomy.md on new files, buglog.json) per OpenWolf
userStories.notes: VocabularyResolver (src/sema/resolve/engine.py, R29-scanned, names no domain literal) orchestrates the §4 stages: candidates.py (1a exact code match in SOURCE vocab via policy.candidate_lookup, R9; no embeddings) -> standardize.py (2: 'Maps to' standard+valid via store.maps_to_targets, relationship/flag from policy, only_valid; self-standard candidate kept; validity-window reduces to invalid_reason IS NULL since ConceptRow has no date cols) -> domain_gate.py (3: policy.in_target_domain). disambiguate.py = single-model Disambiguator protocol + pick_single_survivor default that DECLINES ties (no council). engine_utils.py (NOT scanned; owns every concept_id touch): CodeResolution/ResolveContext + classify_resolution (1 survivor=auto_accepted RESOLVED conf 1.0/Zone-1; 0=NO_MAP conf 1.0/Zone-3; >1 tie=review_pending RESOLVED deterministic min-id placeholder conf 0.5/Zone-2) + build_value_mapping (§1.5(a) row) + build_vocab_lookup_assertion (VOCAB_LOOKUP MappingAssertion, raises on NO_MAP). Code-bearing hot path invokes NO LLM. resolve_and_store is the SOLE value-mapping-store writer (one row per grain incl NO_MAP). 15 hermetic unit tests (FakeVocabStore, TDD-first). Live integration (skip-guarded poc.duckdb): LUAD->45768916; unknown->NO_MAP; OncoTree->SNOMED path coverage RECORDED 63/64 (98.4%), NO_MAP=1 (threshold deferred to US-012). mypy clean (192 files), R29 guard green, coverage 93% repo / 99-100% new modules.

userStories.title: OMOP target via authored manifest + the merged loader (model_role=TARGET)
userStories.description: As the matcher, I want the OMOP condition_occurrence slice loaded as model_role=TARGET via an AUTHORED manifest through the already-merged targets/ framework, so there is a target to map to — writing no new loader/materializer code.
acceptanceCriteria: Build on the MERGED framework — do NOT reimplement: reuse targets/loader.py load_target (:36), ManifestTargetAdapter (adapters/manifest.py:39), the targets/materializer.py -> Neo4j/InMemory writer path, and the `sema target load --manifest` CLI (cli_target.py:33). The only new artifact is the manifest (+ minimal glue if needed); any framework gap -> a minimal fix in targets/, recorded

Author targets/manifests/omop_condition_slice0.yaml (allowlisted OMOP-literal location): manifest_version:1, entity condition_occurrence with its obligation (required_fields incl. condition_concept_id; PK; needed FKs) and a condition_concept_id property carrying a vocabulary_binding {vocabulary: SNOMED target, domain: Condition, require_standard: true, resolver_policy_ref -> the US-004 OMOP/OncoTree policy}

No OMOP literal in src/sema/targets/ code (already true on main); all OMOP specifics live in the authored manifest

integration live test (skip-guarded Neo4j): `sema target load --manifest targets/manifests/omop_condition_slice0.yaml --writer neo4j`; Neo4j visibility query MATCH (e:Entity {model_role:'TARGET'})-[:HAS_PROPERTY]->(p:Property)-[:HAS_VOCABULARY_BINDING]->(vb:VocabularyBinding) WHERE vb.property_name='condition_concept_id' RETURN e.name, vb.domain, vb.require_standard returns one row (condition_occurrence | Condition | true); HAS_OBLIGATION -> :TargetObligation present
R29 guard (US-001) stays green: no 'Maps to'/standard_concept/concept_id/OMOP table/domain/OncoTree literal in src/sema/engine/ or any generic spine module

userStories.notes: Authored src/sema/targets/manifests/omop_condition_slice0.yaml (model_role=TARGET omop.condition_occurrence: condition_concept_id property -> vocabulary_binding {vocabulary: SNOMED target (R9), domain: Condition, require_standard: true, resolver_policy_ref: omop.oncotree_to_snomed_condition (US-004 policy)}; obligation required_fields incl condition_concept_id, NATURAL_KEY PK, FK -> minimal omop.person, domain_constraint -> Condition). NO new loader/materializer code — reused load_target/ManifestTargetAdapter/materializer/`sema target load`; the merged framework already emits VocabularyBindingOp + HAS_VOCABULARY_BINDING, so no targets/ fix needed. TDD: 7 hermetic unit tests (InMemoryGraphWriter; entity/property/obligation/binding ops, HAS_VOCABULARY_BINDING present + NOT HAS_VOCAB_BINDING, normalized binding resolves via resolve_policy() to ResolverPolicy source_vocabulary=OncoTree/target_domain=Condition) written first, failed on missing manifest. Skip-guarded Neo4j integration test runs the AC bridge query (-> condition_occurrence | Condition | true) + HAS_OBLIGATION present. R29 guard scans *.py only so OMOP literals confined to YAML+tests stay clean. pytest 2015 passed/1 skipped; mypy clean (192 files); coverage 93%. NOTE: :Entity has qualified_name not name; :TargetObligation stores payload_json (bug-342).

userStories.title: Slice-0 staging obligation + real PlanAssembler
userStories.description: As the spine, I want a working PlanAssembler that composes assertions against the Slice-0 staging obligation into a MappingPlan with a verdict, so plans assemble and fail as a unit (closes the L4 CONSTANT(NULL) caveat).
acceptanceCriteria: Failing tests first: assembling a resolved condition_concept_id assertion against the staging obligation required={condition_concept_id, resolver_policy_ref, vocab_release} yields PASS; a missing required field yields FAIL; a CONSTANT(NULL)-covered required field yields FAIL; a NO_MAP-pattern FieldMap does NOT cover a required field (§1.5(e)); condition_concept_id stays REQUIRED (not nullable)

Add FieldMap.status: Status (default candidate) per §1.5(d); the assembler sets it from the winning assertion's status; derive_verdict() computes any_review_pending from field-map statuses (replacing the hard-coded False at mapping_plan.py:76); update planner_loader.py serialization (write + read) for the new field

src/sema/resolve/assembler.py: a concrete PlanAssembler; reuse the planner select_winner (mapping_plan.py:112 — NOT winner_selection.py:24) and derive_verdict (mapping_plan.py:62); extend covered_required_fields per §1.5(e); update the PlanAssembler Protocol docstring
The staging obligation is distinct from the production condition_occurrence obligation (no person_id, no dates) and is defined as Slice-0 policy/config, not in engine core
Conforms to PRD §1.5 Contract Spec ((d) status flow + (e) coverage rule); do NOT restate columns — the US-005 drift test enforces the frozen schema

userStories.notes: FieldMap gained status: Status (default candidate) + covers_required_field() (§1.5(e) gate: rejected/NO_MAP/CONSTANT-NULL never cover). MappingPlan.covered_required_fields() filters on it; derive_verdict() computes any_review_pending from field-map statuses (replaces hard-coded False at mapping_plan.py:76; review_pending -> awaiting_review, short-circuits ahead of obligation checks). planner_loader field_map_to_properties/properties_to_field_map round-trip status (read .get default candidate). NEW src/sema/resolve/assembler.py:Slice0PlanAssembler (R29-SCANNED, generic): groups assertions by target_property_ref, select_winner per group (planner mapping_plan.py:112, NOT winner_selection.py), projects winner->FieldMap carrying winner.status, emits RISK_ASSEMBLER_CONFLICT_RESOLVED (Severity.info, non-blocking) on >1 live assertion; raises on empty/all-rejected. Slice-0 staging obligation make_slice0_staging_obligation() in ALLOWLISTED policies/omop.py: required={condition_concept_id, resolver_policy_ref, vocab_release} as dotted RefStr under target.condition_occurrence_staging; NATURAL_KEY PK; distinct from production condition_occurrence (no person_id/dates); condition_concept_id REQUIRED not nullable. Coverage matching is EXACT string equality (target_field_ref needs >=1 dot). 16 hermetic TDD unit tests (tests/unit/test_plan_assembler.py). pytest 2031 passed/1 skipped; mypy clean (193 files); R29 guard clean; coverage 93% repo (planner_loader 100%, omop policy 100%, assembler 97%, field_map 98%, mapping_plan 99%). Pre-existing integration failure bug-347 (graph/loader test_different_sources_coexist) confirmed failing on HEAD too — NOT a US-008 regression.

userStories.title: Deterministic VOCAB_LOOKUP assertion producer + graph edges
userStories.description: As Sema, I want the narrow deterministic producer that turns (target binding + source :ValueSet + resolver) into MappingAssertions and writes the :FieldMap/MAPS_TO/DERIVED_FROM edges, so the mapping exists in the graph.
acceptanceCriteria: Failing tests first: given a source :ValueSet of OncoTree codes, the target condition_concept_id binding (the :VocabularyBinding loaded by US-007 via HAS_VOCABULARY_BINDING, property_name=condition_concept_id), and a mocked resolver, the producer emits one MappingAssertion(pattern=VOCAB_LOOKUP) carrying source_field_ref AND target_property_ref, and materializes a :FieldMap whose target_field_ref is condition_concept_id with MAPS_TO -> target Property and DERIVED_FROM -> source Property (planner_loader.py:101/112)
src/sema/resolve/producer.py (thin); reuses planner_loader.py for the edge writes

No MappingProposal. The producer emits MappingAssertions and writes the graph edges; it READS per-code decisions from the value-mapping store (written solely by US-006 — the producer never writes the store). Chain is MappingAssertion -> FieldMap -> MappingPlan only

integration live test (Neo4j): run the producer over a real study's ONCOTREE_CODE :ValueSet + the US-007 binding; Neo4j bridge query MATCH (src:Property {model_role:'SOURCE'})<-[:DERIVED_FROM]-(fm:FieldMap)-[:MAPS_TO]->(tgt:Property {model_role:'TARGET'}) RETURN src.name, fm.pattern, tgt.name returns one row (ONCOTREE_CODE | VOCAB_LOOKUP | condition_concept_id); assert MAPS_TO originates at the :FieldMap, never a source :Property
R29 guard (US-001) stays green: no 'Maps to'/standard_concept/concept_id/OMOP table/domain/OncoTree literal in src/sema/engine/ or any generic spine module
Strict TDD: failing test(s) written first, failing for the right reason

userStories.title: Transform compiler (MappingPlan -> SQLGlot -> staging write)
userStories.description: As Sema, I want the compiler that renders a MappingPlan to SQL via SQLGlot and writes the §1.5(b) staging artifact on both DuckDB and Databricks, so the pipeline actually moves rows. Hard-blocked on the frozen store columns (US-005).
acceptanceCriteria: Failing tests first: compiling a VOCAB_LOOKUP MappingPlan builds a SQLGlot AST that INLINES resolved decisions as JOIN (VALUES ('LUAD', 4314337), ...) AS m(code, concept_id) and renders to BOTH DuckDB and Databricks dialects; the AST is built once and dialect-rendered (no per-dialect string templates)
src/sema/compile/ (compiler.py + *_utils.py): dispatch on (MappingPattern, TargetArtifactKind); Slice 0 implements VOCAB_LOOKUP -> TABLE_ROW

Writes the §1.5(b) staging table with policy-owned generic column names; src/sema/compile/ must NOT contain the literals source_oncotree_code or condition_concept_id (R29, verified by US-001); showcase names come from the US-004 policy / US-007 manifest; no person_id, no synthetic person row, no FK closure
<target_concept_column> is NULL for NO_MAP-status rows (§1.5(b) projection)
Idempotency = atomic temp-table build + swap, scoped on (source_schema, source_table); re-running a study reproduces byte-identical staging rows; no general transaction coordinator

integration live test (~/.sema/poc.duckdb): compile + execute writing a temp staging table; assert row count = source ONCOTREE_CODE row count, the join populated <target_concept_column>, and NO_MAP rows are NULL there; AND render the Databricks dialect SQL and assert it parses
Conforms to PRD §1.5 Contract Spec ((b) staging table); do NOT restate columns — the US-005 drift test enforces the frozen schema

userStories.title: Gate D-lite staging QA
userStories.description: As the team, I want a staging QA check (row count, null-rate on the resolved concept column, NO_MAP accounting vs the gold set), so a bad staging table is caught before it is called 'done'.
acceptanceCriteria: Failing tests first: a staging table with the wrong row count, an unexpected null-rate on <target_concept_column> (accounting for legitimate NO_MAP NULLs), or NO_MAP counts that disagree with the gold set each FAIL the check with a structured reason; a clean table PASSES

src/sema/compile/staging_qa.py (or eval/): computes the three checks; null-rate reconciled against resolution_status (NO_MAP rows are expected NULLs, not defects); NO_MAP accounting reconciled against the US-002 gold set (a code the gold set says is RESOLVED but whose staging row is NO_MAP is reported, not silently dropped)
integration live test against the US-010 DuckDB staging table: QA passes on a correct run and fails on a deliberately corrupted copy

userStories.title: Mapping eval report (confusion matrix vs gold set, thresholds)
userStories.description: As the team, I want the eval harness (US-002) run end-to-end over the real resolved decisions to produce the §1.5(f) metrics with acceptance thresholds, so 'reports precision/recall' is not hand-wavy.
acceptanceCriteria: Failing tests first: given a synthetic decision set + gold set, the report computes mapped_precision, mapped_recall, auto_resolution_rate, and no_map_accuracy per §1.5(f), with no_map_accuracy reported SEPARATELY, at distinct-code, row-weighted, and per-frequency-bucket granularity

src/sema/eval/mapping_report.py (+ *_utils.py): emits a structured report (JSON + human summary) reading the value-mapping store (US-005) and the gold set; reuses the US-002 metric module (no re-derived math)

Thresholds asserted in the integration live run (real resolved decisions): >=95% mapped_precision AND >=70% auto_resolution_rate — ONLY when labelled gold coverage = 100% of observed distinct codes; on a subset the report says 'provisional — not accepted'; below either threshold at full coverage: 'running, not accepted'

Depends on the US-002 HUMAN-label checkpoint: Ralph CANNOT self-certify acceptance — at less than human-labelled 100% coverage emit 'provisional — not accepted' and surface the gap; never flip US-013 acceptance on self-generated labels
The report explicitly states that Slice 0's ~100% precision is STRUCTURAL (a deterministic exact-code walk) and does NOT validate the product precision approach on the ambiguous tail
Conforms to PRD §1.5 Contract Spec ((f) metrics); do NOT restate columns — the US-005 drift test enforces the frozen schema
R29 guard (US-001) stays green: no 'Maps to'/standard_concept/concept_id/OMOP table/domain/OncoTree literal in src/sema/engine/ or any generic spine module

userStories.description: As the team, I want the whole resolve -> produce -> assemble -> compile -> staging-write -> QA -> eval chain exercised once end-to-end on local DuckDB, so interface drift between independently-built units is caught before the live Databricks gate.
acceptanceCriteria: Extend cli.py with `sema fit` (or sema resolve + sema materialize) running the full chain for a given study + target binding against the DuckDB backend. The TARGET load is a prerequisite performed by the existing `sema target load` (US-007); `sema fit` consumes the already-materialized TARGET graph/binding rather than re-loading it

userStories.title: Generality counter-example: no-vocab manifest target (R29 boundary proof)
userStories.description: As a general engine, I want one non-OMOP target — a plain analytics table with no vocabulary at all — to flow through the same target-loader -> assembler -> compiler -> staging-write with zero concept resolution, so the engine/policy boundary is proven real, not asserted.
acceptanceCriteria: Failing tests first: a SECOND authored manifest targets/manifests/dim_customer.yaml for a curated dim_customer target declaring obligations (required columns, PK, types) but with NO vocabulary_binding; loaded via the SAME load_target / ManifestTargetAdapter (US-007). A CSV source (customers.csv) flows through assembler -> compiler; the resolver step is SKIPPED entirely; every field map uses an existing MappingPattern — DIRECT_COPY (copy/rename) and DERIVED (a cast via DerivedExpression) and/or CONSTANT — with no concept_id, no 'Maps to', no domain gate. Do NOT add RENAME/TYPE_CAST enum members
Loading the no-vocab manifest writes NO :VocabularyBinding node and NO HAS_VOCABULARY_BINDING edge — assert their absence (the positive complement of US-007)

The same target loader (US-007), assembler (US-008), and compiler (US-010) handle this target WITHOUT OMOP-shaped branches — if any needs an OMOP-specific code path, that is the bug this story exists to catch

integration live test: load dim_customer.yaml (hermetic in-memory writer) and compile + execute the dim_customer write against DuckDB from a fixture CSV; assert the rows materialize with no vocabulary columns and the R29 guard (US-001) stays green
R29 guard (US-001) stays green: no 'Maps to'/standard_concept/concept_id/OMOP table/domain/OncoTree literal in src/sema/engine/ or any generic spine module
